messages.content:
Three ants walk on a thin rod 100 cm long.
When an ant reaches either end of the rod it falls off. When two ants meet,
they BOUNCE: both instantly reverse direction, and each keeps its own speed.

Starting positions and velocities (positive = toward the 100 cm end):

- Ant A: at 10 cm, moving +3 cm/s
- Ant B: at 40 cm, moving -1 cm/s
- Ant C: at 80 cm, moving -2 cm/s

After how many seconds does the LAST ant fall off the rod? Give the exact
number of seconds.


When you are finished, output your final answer on its own line, as the last line of your response, in the exact format:
ANSWER: <your answer>

text:
The collisions are simulated directly using the given bounce rule (each ant reverses its own velocity upon meeting, while retaining its speed).

- Ants A and B approach each other and collide after \(30/4 = 15/2\) seconds at position \(32.5\). Their velocities become \(-3\) and \(+1\), respectively. At this instant ant C is at position \(65\).
- Ant A (now moving left) reaches the left end after an additional \(65/6\) seconds and falls off at total time \(55/3\). Simultaneously, ants B and C collide at position \(130/3\). Their velocities become \(-1\) and \(+2\), respectively.
- After this second collision the ants move apart. Ant C reaches the right end after an additional \(85/3\) seconds and falls off at total time \(140/3\). Ant B reaches the left end after an additional \(130/3\) seconds and falls off at total time \(185/3\).

No further collisions occur. The latest fall-off time is therefore \(185/3\).

ANSWER: 185/3
